target.label: 6-DOF Industrial Arm educational mockup
target.scope: design-thinking and 3D-printable assembly package before STL generation
target.not_for: certified industrial robot, payload-rated arm, collaborative safety certification, or precision automation claims

primary_metric.name: assembly_serviceability_efficiency
primary_metric.definition: Reduce assembly effort while preserving access for J1-J6 axis inspection, reducer/bearing dummies, encoder covers, cable replacement, wrist service, and tool flange swaps.
primary_metric.failure_rule: Part count reduction fails if joint cartridges, axis datums, cable spine, or tool flange access are blocked.

existing_bom.label: J1 base rotary joint
existing_bom.role: base mounting datum and first rotation axis
existing_bom.service_access: J1 axis and base fasteners must remain visible

existing_bom.label: J2 shoulder joint
existing_bom.role: shoulder pitch axis and main torque path
existing_bom.service_access: shoulder cartridge and encoder cover must remain removable

existing_bom.label: upper arm link
existing_bom.role: hollow structural link between J2 and J3
existing_bom.service_access: internal cable route must remain inspectable

existing_bom.label: J3 elbow joint
existing_bom.role: elbow pitch axis and link transition
existing_bom.service_access: elbow reducer and axis datum must remain visible

existing_bom.label: forearm and J4 wrist roll link
existing_bom.role: forearm structure and wrist roll routing
existing_bom.service_access: wrist cable pass-through must remain open

existing_bom.label: J5 wrist pitch joint
existing_bom.service_access: wrist pitch cover must remain removable

existing_bom.label: J6 wrist roll joint
existing_bom.role: final rotation axis and tool flange driver
existing_bom.service_access: J6 axis and tool mounting pattern must remain inspectable

existing_bom.label: tool flange and changer
existing_bom.service_access: tool latch and utility ports must remain external

existing_bom.label: external cable management spine
existing_bom.role: replaceable cable carrier from base to wrist
existing_bom.service_access: cable spine must be replaceable without opening joint cartridges

redesigned_bom.label: serviceable base and shoulder cartridge set
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG base shell with removable J1/J2 cartridge covers and visible axis datum rings

redesigned_bom.label: hollow upper and forearm link pair with open cable channel
redesigned_bom.printable_as: two PETG hollow links with integrated cable channel and datum labels

redesigned_bom.label: removable elbow and wrist joint module set
redesigned_bom.printable_as: separate J3/J5/J6 service cartridges with bearing/reducer dummy access covers

redesigned_bom.label: tool flange service module
redesigned_bom.printable_as: replaceable PETG tool flange with visible ISO-style bolt pattern and utility pass-through guide

redesigned_bom.label: external replaceable cable spine
redesigned_bom.printable_as: clip-on PETG/TPU cable carrier that remains outside joint cartridges

integration_decisions.decision: integrate base shell with J1/J2 datum labels but keep cartridges removable
integration_decisions.expected_gain: reduces base cover alignment operations without trapping joint service items
integration_decisions.guardrail: J1 and J2 axis rings remain visible

integration_decisions.decision: integrate hollow links with open cable channels
integration_decisions.expected_gain: removes loose covers and improves cable path visibility
integration_decisions.guardrail: cable spine remains replaceable and not buried inside closed links

integration_decisions.decision: keep J3/J5/J6 as removable joint cartridges
integration_decisions.expected_gain: preserves reducer, bearing, encoder, and wrist service access
integration_decisions.guardrail: joint cartridges can be removed without cutting printed links

integration_decisions.decision: keep tool flange as replaceable service module
integration_decisions.expected_gain: tool interface can change without rebuilding wrist stack
integration_decisions.guardrail: tool latch and utility ports remain external

integration_decisions.expected_gain: cable replacement becomes visible and teachable
integration_decisions.guardrail: spine clips on from outside and does not share joint cartridge fasteners

removed_operations: route cables through hidden link cavities
open joint cartridges to replace cable spine
remove wrist stack to inspect tool utility path
re-square J2/J3 link covers after cable service

print_strategy.orientation: base flange on bed, J1/J2 axis rings upward
print_strategy.reason: preserve base mounting datum and visible shoulder axis rings

print_strategy.orientation: links printed on side with cable channel upward
print_strategy.reason: protect hollow link walls and leave cable channel clean
preserve J3/J5/J6 datum faces and access cover geometry
keep bolt pattern face flat and readable

print_strategy.orientation: cable spine clips flat, TPU pads face-down
print_strategy.reason: external cable carrier should print quickly and remain replaceable

assembly_sequence: Print base/shoulder cartridge set with visible J1/J2 datum rings.
Print hollow upper and forearm link pair with open cable channels.
Print external cable spine and TPU cable pads.
Attach base shell to display mounting plate and verify J1 datum ring.
Install J2 shoulder cartridge cover without closing cable entry.
Attach upper arm link to J2 datum face.
Install J3 elbow cartridge between upper and forearm links.
Attach forearm link and verify cable pass-through is open.
Install J5/J6 wrist cartridges as removable modules.
Attach tool flange service module and verify bolt pattern visibility.
Clip external cable spine from base to wrist.
Check J1-J6 axis labels, cartridge removal, and cable replacement path.
Perform final service access check for joint covers, tool flange, and cable spine.

requirements: Create a 3D-printable 6-DOF robot arm educational mockup design package.
Reduce assembly steps while preserving J1-J6 axis, joint cartridge, cable, wrist, and tool flange service access.
Keep reducer/bearing/encoder dummy access covers and cable spine replaceable.
Prepare geometry intent for later STL generation, not a payload-rated industrial robot.

children.name: serviceable base and shoulder cartridge set
hollow upper and forearm link pair with open cable channel
removable elbow and wrist joint module set

args.text: J1 J2 J3 J4 J5 J6 AXES

cad_brief.name: robot_arm_serviceability_redesign_v1
cad_brief.build_direction: base flange on bed; links on side with channels upward; joint axis faces upward; tool flange face on bed

surface.post_process: deburr joint cartridge covers, cable clips, and tool flange holes

verify.check: existing and redesigned BOM both present in design package

verify.value: J1/J2/J3/J5/J6 visible; J4 cable pass-through retained in forearm
J2, J3, J5, J6 cartridge covers removable
external cable spine clips and wrist loop visible
visible bolt pattern and utility pass-through guide

risk.desc: Over-integration of joint cartridges into links may block reducer and encoder dummy access.
risk.mit: Keep J2/J3/J5/J6 as removable cartridge covers.

risk.desc: Axis alignment may be lost if J2/J3 datum faces are hidden by link covers.
risk.mit: Expose axis rings and engrave J1-J6 datum labels.

risk.desc: Cable replacement access may degrade if cable spine is routed inside closed links.
risk.mit: Use external replaceable cable spine and open link channels.

risk.desc: Tool flange service may be blocked if flange and wrist shell are one piece.
risk.mit: Keep tool flange as separate service module.

risk.desc: Tall link prints may warp and disturb cartridge fit.
risk.mit: Print links on side, keep channels upward, and verify cartridge clearance.

print_profile.material: PETG structural parts, TPU cable pads
print_profile.infill: 20% gyroid for links and base, 30% for joint cartridges, 15% for cable spine
print_profile.orientation: base flange on bed; links on side with cable channels upward; joint axis faces upward; tool flange face on bed

quality_scenarios.case: joint integrated into link reduces part count but blocks reducer and encoder access
hollow link and cable channel integrated while joint cartridges remain removable
quality_scenarios.reason: assembly is simplified without hiding axes or service modules

quality_scenarios.case: print is easy but J2/J3 axis datum faces disappear
quality_scenarios.reason: axis alignment and calibration marks must remain inspectable
